question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user inject an arbitrarily large value for `maxTransactionFee` into the `PublishScenarioProperties.properties` map (deserialized via `OBJECT_MAPPER.convertValue()` in `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.convert()`) to cause the monitor to submit transactions with fees far exceeding the design-intended ceiling, draining the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the absence of an upper-bound `@Max` constraint on `maxTransactionFee` in `AccountCreateTransactionSupplier` (only `@Min(1)` is enforced) to set `maxTransactionFee` to `Long.MAX_VALUE`, causing `AccountCreateTransaction.setMaxTransactionFee(Hbar.fromTinybars(maxTransactionFee))` to authorize a fee of ~92 million HBAR per transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ACCEPT_CASE_INSENSITIVE_PROPERTIES = true` setting in `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator`'s `OBJECT_MAPPER` to bypass property-name validation by submitting `MaxTransactionFee`, `MAXTRANSACTIONFEE`, or `max_transaction_fee` as a key in the scenario `properties` map, causing the fee to be set outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `FAIL_ON_UNKNOWN_PROPERTIES = false` setting in `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator`'s `OBJECT_MAPPER` to silently inject unrecognized fee-related properties alongside `maxTransactionFee`, causing the supplier to use an unvalidated fee value outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a scenario `properties` map entry where `maxTransactionFee` is set to `0` (bypassing the `@Min(1)` constraint via type coercion or null injection through Jackson deserialization), causing `Hbar.fromTinybars(0)` to be set and transactions to be submitted with no fee cap, leading to unexpected network behavior?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `.retry()` operator in the `publish()` Flux pipeline — which retries indefinitely on any error — combined with an inflated `maxTransactionFee`, to cause repeated high-fee transaction submissions that continuously drain the operator account beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the `tps` field in `PublishScenarioProperties` to an extremely high value (no upper bound enforced) combined with an elevated `maxTransactionFee`, causing the `publish()` Flux to generate and submit transactions at a rate that multiplies fee expenditure far beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `onErrorContinue(PublishException.class, (t, r) -> publishMetrics.onError((PublishException) t))` handler in `publish()` to suppress fee-related errors and allow the pipeline to continue submitting transactions with out-of-bounds fees without halting?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `onErrorContinue((t, r) -> log.error(...))` catch-all in `publish()` to silently swallow validation exceptions thrown when `maxTransactionFee` exceeds design parameters, allowing the pipeline to continue with an unvalidated fee?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a negative value for `maxTransactionFee` via the `properties` map (exploiting Jackson's numeric type handling before `@Min(1)` validation fires), causing `Hbar.fromTinybars()` to receive a negative value and produce an undefined fee state outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ExpressionConverter.convert()` call in `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.convert()` to inject a computed expression (e.g., a SpEL or EL expression) as the value of `maxTransactionFee` in the `properties` map, causing the fee to be evaluated to an arbitrary value outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ScenarioPropertiesAggregator.aggregateProperties()` call in `ConfigurableTransactionGenerator.convert()` to merge or override the `maxTransactionFee` property with an attacker-controlled value, bypassing the supplier's default fee constraints?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user set `maxTransactionFee` to `1_000_000_000` (the default for `AccountCreateTransactionSupplier`) and simultaneously set `clients` to a large value in `PublishProperties`, causing `publish()` to run `publishProperties.getClients()` parallel threads each submitting max-fee transactions, multiplying total fee expenditure beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `parallel(publishProperties.getClients())` and `runOn(Schedulers.newParallel(...))` calls in `publish()` to amplify the rate of high-fee transaction submissions by inflating the `clients` property, causing fees to be consumed at a rate outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `parallel(publishProperties.getResponseThreads())` stage in `publish()` combined with an elevated `maxTransactionFee` to increase the throughput of fee-bearing transactions processed per second beyond design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user set `recordPercent = 1.0` in `PublishScenarioProperties` to force every transaction to trigger `processTransactionResponse()` with `setQueryPayment(Hbar.from(1, HbarUnit.HBAR))` in `TransactionPublisher`, causing an additional hardcoded 1 HBAR fee per transaction on top of the `maxTransactionFee`, effectively doubling fee expenditure outside design parameters?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user set both `receiptPercent = 1.0` and `recordPercent = 1.0` in `PublishScenarioProperties` to trigger both receipt and record queries per transaction in `processTransactionResponse()`, stacking additional fee payments beyond the `maxTransactionFee` design parameter?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ScheduleCreateTransactionSupplier.get()` method — which sets `maxTransactionFee` on both the inner `TransferTransaction` and the outer `ScheduleCreateTransaction` — by injecting an elevated `maxTransactionFee` value, causing double the intended fee authorization per scheduled transaction?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `ConsensusSubmitMessageTransactionSupplier`'s `maxTransactionFee` field (default `4_000_000`, only `@Min(1)` enforced) by injecting a value of `1_000_000_000` via the `properties` map, causing `TopicMessageSubmitTransaction.setMaxTransactionFee()` to authorize 250x the intended fee?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `CryptoTransferTransactionSupplier`'s `maxTransactionFee` field (default `10_000_000`, only `@Min(1)` enforced) by injecting `Long.MAX_VALUE` via the `properties` map, causing `TransferTransaction.setMaxTransactionFee(Hbar.fromTinybars(Long.MAX_VALUE))` to authorize an astronomically large fee?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `TokenCreateTransactionSupplier`'s `maxTransactionFee` field (default `1_000_000_000`, only `@Min(1)` enforced) by injecting a value exceeding the operator's balance, causing `TokenCreateTransaction.setMaxTransactionFee()` to authorize a fee that drains the operator account?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `flatMapIterable(Function.identity())` step in `publish()` to emit a large batch of `PublishRequest` objects each carrying an inflated `maxTransactionFee`, causing the parallel publisher threads to simultaneously submit many high-fee transactions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `generate(sink -> sink.next(transactionGenerator.next(0)))` infinite generator in `publish()` — which never terminates unless an exception is thrown — combined with an elevated `maxTransactionFee`, to cause continuous high-fee transaction submission indefinitely?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `Hooks.onErrorDropped` static initializer in `MonitorConfiguration` — which suppresses non-`CompletionException` errors — to hide fee-related SDK exceptions that would otherwise alert operators to out-of-bounds fee submissions?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/config/MonitorConfiguration.java -> Scope: Medium: Modification of transaction fees outside of design parameters'] [Function: publish()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the `doFinally(s -> log.warn(...))` handler in `publish()` — which only logs a warning when the publisher stops — to ensure that fee-related anomalies are not surfaced as errors, allowing out-of-bounds fee transactions to proceed silently?
